races.race_name: BJ Llewellyn Racing In Memory Of Gwilym Llewellyn Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Voix De Bocelli
runners.spotlight: String of solid efforts around this time last year but he's generally struggled under both codes since; needs to bounce back under Henry Callan, who's an amazing 7-16 this year and 14-44 overall.
runners.comment: String of solid efforts this time last year but rather struggled under both codes since

runners.name: Louie The Legend
runners.spotlight: Well below market expectations over hurdles last time (potential stamina issues but has never been the most consistent) and returns to the Flat on a handy mark; course winner as a 2yo (good to soft) and he's considered under Fletcher Yarham.
runners.comment: Returns to the Flat on a handy mark; one to consider under Fletcher Yarham

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who was allowed his own way in front when a beaten favourite on his first crack at 0-50 company (Tapeta) a fortnight ago; generally consistent but had plenty of chances and others look stronger.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden; generally consistent but had plenty of chances and others look stronger

runners.name: Summer Evening
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden (6f-1m4f) who's been given a ten-week break since running as well as could have been expected in novice company; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Break since running as well as could have been expected in a novice; be a surprise winner

runners.name: The Pug
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent for three previous yards, winning just once since 2023; beaten a long way back on turf five weeks ago and isn't obviously fancied for new trainer.
runners.comment: Inconsistent for three previous yards, winning just once since 2023; hard to fancy

runners.name: Alazwar
runners.spotlight: Trainer-jockey combination won this last year and this gelding is on a career-low mark (won for Brodie Hampson last June off 9lb higher); needs to bounce back from a few poor efforts, though, and may just be on the downgrade now; headgear comes off.
runners.comment: On a career-low mark (won for Brodie Hampson last June off 9lb higher); has to bounce back

runners.name: Showmedemoney
runners.spotlight: Again no sign of the tongue-tie worn for most wins as he has a rare run away from the AW; bit better at Lingfield recently on his second run for this yard but needs more back on turf.
runners.comment: Bit better on his second run for this yard last time but needs more back on turf

runners.name: Pride Of Nepal
runners.spotlight: Frequent slow starter who's enjoyed a revival since returned to turf, minus the tongue-tie and headgear; looked to have a bit more in hand than the final margin at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) off 3lb lower last time and holds obvious claims.
runners.comment: Bit more in hand than the final margin off 3lb lower last time and holds obvious claims

runners.name: Tiger Beetle
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade for this yard since missing a year; running fairly well on Wolverhampton AW (always liked it there) of late, often in races not run to suit, but only holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Been on the downgrade for this yard since missing a year; only holds each-way claims

runners.name: Olivia Jane
runners.spotlight: On her third yard and back from ten months off; string of poor efforts last year and couldn't be backed unless there's serious support back at 1m2f and tried in a third form of headgear.
runners.comment: On her third yard and back from ten months off; watch the market

runners.name: Pomeriggio
runners.spotlight: Regressive, ex-Irish mare who's been given five months off since a couple of poor efforts on the AW in the winter; little to recommend her this time.
runners.comment: Regressive, ex-Irish mare; little to recommend her back from five months off

races.race_name: BJ Llewellyn Racing In Memory Of Gwilym Llewellyn Amateur Jockeys' Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Little She
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's been beaten several times at short prices, including down to 0-55 company last time; that effort was better than it looked (best of those to race with the pace) and she's capable if the weather holds (withdrawn on good to soft last summer).
runners.comment: Maiden; beaten several times at short prices but latest third better than it looked

runners.name: Palazzo Persico
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner for Ed Dunlop; lost his way in Ireland earlier in the year and, although potentially well treated if the change of scenery has helped, he's hard to recommend this time.
runners.comment: Lost his way in Ireland earlier in the year; well treated if change of scenery has helped

runners.name: Change Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who has plenty of short-priced defeats to go with his sole win; been running no more than fairly well in 0-50 company this year, including back on turf last time.
runners.comment: Running no more than fairly in 0-50 company this year, including back on turf last time

runners.name: Artavian
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner in autumn 2024 who proved patchy during a limited campaign last year (fair second for Frederick Tett latterly); inclind to pull hard on comeback (Tapeta) and should fare better in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Inclind to pull hard on comeback (Tapeta); should fare better in a first-time tongue-tie
runners.owner: G E Bassett & P R Chamings

runners.name: Ignition
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's often been turned over at short prices and generally been kept to the AW (achieved little in three runs on turf); others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Generally kept to the AW (achieved little in three runs on turf); others make more appeal

runners.name: San Francisco Bay
runners.spotlight: May have needed his recent comeback from seven months off and is back on a competitive mark for a yard in fair form of late; one to consider in a race lacking depth.
runners.comment: Back on a competitive mark for a yard in fair form of late; one to consider

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who boasted a poor strike-rate prior to winning three round Bath in recent weeks, including for Louise Chance last month; different type of course today but showed the right attitude last time and has been found a weak-looking race up 3lb.
runners.comment: Showed the right attitude last time and has been found a weak-looking race up 3lb

runners.name: Offiah's Boy
runners.spotlight: Won four times on good to firm/firm ground last summer, latterly off a 1lb higher mark; lost his way since a fall over hurdles last autumn and has achieved little on going that includes the word 'soft'; needs to bounce back following a layoff.
runners.comment: Lost his way since a fall over hurdles last autumn and needs the ground to dry out

runners.name: Resilient Lady
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish placed after 14 starts and was beaten a long way back on turf at Lingfield in May.
runners.comment: Still to finish placed after 14 starts and will do well to alter that

runners.name: Bloodhound
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who did his racing in the winter over further on the AW; not obviously fancied back from a few months off.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden; not obviously fancied back from a few months off

runners.name: Thursday
runners.spotlight: Finished last in two runs back having missed a little over a year; handicapper proving slow to relent and first-time headgear will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: Handicapper proving slow to relent and first-time headgear will need to make a difference

races.race_name: Susan Wastie Memorial / EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Eabha
runners.spotlight: Yard enjoying a good season with 2yos (good record with them here down the years) and among the winners of late; this filly, whose pedigree is a mix of speed and stamina, sets the standard on her debut third at Newbury (6.5f) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on her debut third at Newbury (6.5f) a fortnight ago (Tall Trees fifth)

runners.name: Tall Trees
runners.spotlight: Started a big price when behind Eabha at Newbury a fortnight ago; shaped with a deal of promise there, having looked clueless early, and she'll have learned plenty from that; trainer boasts a decent record with 2yos here (14-71).
runners.comment: Deal of promise behind Eabha on debut, having looked clueless; will have learned plenty

runners.name: Havananother
runners.spotlight: £55,000 yearling whose dam won over 5f as a 3yo for Richard Hannon and who holds a Super Sprint entry next month; trainer boasts a much better strike-rate with 2yos on the AW than turf, including this season.
runners.comment: £55,000 yearling; trainer boasts a much better strike-rate with 2yos on the AW than turf

runners.name: Miss Kodi
runners.spotlight: 95,000euros yearling who's the first foal of an unraced half-sister to 2yo Group 1 winner Arcano; trainer's juveniles are quite capable of winning on debut and he's a decent 9-47 with them here down the years; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 95,000euros yearling; trainer's juveniles are quite capable of winning on debut

runners.name: Spirit Tango
runners.spotlight: 185,000euros foal, 475,000gns yearling who's a half-sister to Listed winner Majestic Dawn, useful sprinter Rousing Encore and a couple of lesser winners; likely type on paper.
runners.comment: 185,000euros foal, 475,000gns yearling; interesting newcomer from a powerful yard

runners.name: Absolute Diamond
runners.spotlight: £38,000 yearling, £12,000 2yo (April breeze-up) who's the third foal of a French 6f winner; trainer enjoyed a fair spring with juveniles and the market will show what's expected.
runners.comment: £12,000 2yo (April breeze-up); trainer enjoyed a fair spring with 2yos; market will guide

runners.name: Sayidah Ardad
runners.spotlight: Hung right and lost her action on debut for Richard Hughes last month; much better from the front last time, despite again wandering, but nurseries may be her time to shine, having since been picked up for 7,500gns.
runners.comment: Better last time but nurseries may be her time to shine (since picked up for 7,500gns)

runners.name: Dreams Galore
runners.spotlight: 66-1 for her 5f Nottingham debut recently, when slowly away and never involved; would need a big step forward to take this.
runners.comment: 66-1 for her 5f Nottingham debut recently, when slowly away and never involved

races.race_name: Tom Malone Bloodstock Supports Jamie's Farm Handicap

runners.name: Sonic Si
runners.spotlight: Regressive on the AW earlier in the year for this yard, having missed a year, but the return to turf and drop to 0-55 company did the trick under Rossa Ryan last time; going back up 7lb will test him but he's only had a handful of starts on grass and may well be up to it.
runners.comment: Back up 7lb for recent win but had just a few starts on grass and may well be up to it

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: Just a nursery win for another yard to her name and expensive to follow this year, getting turned over as favourite three times; consistent enough but been nudged up the weights in defeat and is having just a second try at 5f.
runners.comment: Consistent but nudged up the weights in defeat and is having just a second try at 5f

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Ultra-consistent, straightforward 5f specialist who's fine over an undulating track (C&D winner last June; two of these behind); handicapper knows where he is but having 7lb claimer Alexandra McDonnell (ridden three winners for the yard) up won't hurt.
runners.comment: Consistent, straightforward 5f specialist who's fine over an undulating track;  player

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: Seven-time course winner (4-12 over this trip); has been running over 7f of late but should be all right with the return to 5f and is back at the right sort of level off her last winning mark; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Seven-time course winner (4-12 over this trip); enters calculations back down in trip

runners.name: Savannah Smiles
runners.spotlight: A few near-misses to go with her C&D win and she bounced back last time from a string of poor efforts on her first start since undergoing wind surgery; on a fair mark if she builds on that.
runners.comment: A few near-misses to go with her C&D win; better last time following wind surgery; claims

runners.name: Fletcher's Flight
runners.spotlight: Popped up from nowhere when winning in a first-time tongue-tie over C&D (soft) nine days ago; well drawn as it turned out that day and recent history suggests he's no certainty to repeat the effort, back up 4lb and into a better race.
runners.comment: Well drawn over C&D latest; no certainty to repeat it, back up 4lb in a better race

runners.name: Some Nightmare
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner who raced on the 'wrong' side of the course when fifth behind Fletcher's Flight here nine days ago; 5lb better off today, with Myla Coppins taking off another 7lb, and there's every chance he turns things round.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; drawn out of it here last time; should fare much better
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould & Partner

races.race_name: Menzies Recovery Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Ravenglass
runners.spotlight: C&D win last May came off a 1lb higher mark; promising seasonal return in April but two lesser runs have followed; stable also run Sioux Warrior.
runners.comment: C&D win last May came off 1lb higher; needs to bounce back from a couple of modest efforts

runners.name: Hint Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW this year and he carried on the good work back on turf when finishing second of nine at Nottingham 12 days ago (6f, good); the return to 7f looks a positive and he looks a strong contender.
runners.comment: Had a good year on AW & also ran well back on turf latest; return to 7f a plus; contender

runners.name: Terries Royale
runners.spotlight: Attracted market support at Bath on Saturday (tongue tied for the first time) but fared no better, dropping away in the closing stages; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Dropped away in the closing stages at Bath on Saturday; not a solid option

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: 6f wins on good and good to firm in the spring (second and third runs for James Owen); beaten favourite twice since and now moves back up to 7f; drying ground a plus for her.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins this spring but beaten favourite on last two starts; now steps back up to 7f

runners.name: Future Cutlet
runners.spotlight: Dropped a long way in the weights since joining this yard and not really looked like capitalising; visor tried for the first time; not solid but a revival wouldn't be a total shock (attracted some support last time).
runners.comment: Tumbling down the weights and attracted some support last time; new headgear given a go

runners.name: Sioux Warrior
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but he went close over 7f at Wolverhampton in March and his Bath third last month (5.7f, good to firm) was another sound effort; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but stays 7f and he's been placed on his last two starts; each-way shout

runners.name: Havanita
runners.spotlight: Little solid form to her name and she was tailed off at Lingfield (7f, good) on her seasonal return four weeks ago; now tried in a visor.
runners.comment: Little solid form, including on reappearance, and a new visor isn't enough to tempt.
runners.owner: Mrs R Lyon & P R Chamings

runners.name: Belltony
runners.spotlight: Big prices and modest form in three Wolverhampton runs this winter (7f-8.6f); this is more realistic but he's still not easily recommended.
runners.comment: First realistic assignment but would want to see market support before getting involved

runners.name: The Organiser
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower than when winning at Brighton last September (6f, soft); solid second, albeit put in his place by the winner, at Lingfield four weeks ago (7f, good); drying conditions may suit others more.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but drying conditions will suit others more

races.race_name: Engstaff-In Birthday Handicap

runners.name: Too Much Trevor
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 31st attempt when beating Punchbowl Flyer over C&D nine days ago (soft; Autumn Angel fourth); 2lb worse off with the runner-up, who did best of those racing stands' side, and wouldn't be sure to confirm the placings.
runners.comment: 31st time lucky when beating Punchbowl Flyer over C&D nine days ago; contender

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Just one win in the last five years but it came over C&D; back to form when second to Too Much Trevor over C&D nine days ago, doing comfortably best of the stands' side group, and 2lb better off today; good chance of turning the tables.
runners.comment: Back to form when second to Too Much Trevor here nine days ago (did best on stands' side)

runners.name: Blue Point Express
runners.spotlight: 7f win on soft ground here last September; 2lb lower today and she should be sharper with last month's reappearance behind her; others look safer.
runners.comment: 7f win here (soft) last September; 2lb lower today but needs to raise her game

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways when leading late in a C&D handicap last month (good to soft); not quite so good when fourth to Too Much Trevor nine days ago but it was still a fair effort; shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: C&D win last month; fourth to Too Much Trevor back here nine days ago; should be involved

runners.name: Whitesnake
runners.spotlight: Modest form in her three runs in 6f novice/maiden company (peak RPR of 45); Rossa Ryan booked for her handicap debut and this isn't a deep race; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Modest form in 3 maiden/novice runs; Rossa Ryan booked; check betting now handicapping

runners.name: Bama Lama
runners.spotlight: Mudlark who starts her campaign off a lowly mark; she has a nine-month absence to overcome, however, and that hasn't been the time to catch her in previous seasons.
runners.comment: Mudlark who lurks on a dangerous mark; record fresh doesn't suggest she's the answer here

runners.name: Poet
runners.spotlight: Has failed to beat a rival in his last eight races; hard to fancy back from a three-month break.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten a rival in his last eight starts; hard to fancy

races.race_name: opx.io Operating System For Continuous Improvement Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qual') (Div I)

runners.name: Judge Frank
runners.spotlight: Well held all four runs this season but has had excuses with one thing and another, latterly when drawn out of it over C&D nine days ago (among the favourites); has gone well here previously and is one to consider off a career-low mark.
runners.comment: Well held all four runs this season but has had excuses with one thing and another; player

runners.name: Baynoona
runners.spotlight: Second at Bath in the spring before appearing not to see out 1m2f on latest outing (not for the first time); last autumn's win (good to soft) came off a similar mark and it wouldn't be a shock were she to pop up.
runners.comment: Last autumn's win (good to soft) was off a similar mark; no great shock were she to pop up
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Ajrad
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D reads 1112; getting on and has not been at his best this year but this task is easier, down in class having been dropped another 2lb; ought to fare much better.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 1112; down in class having been dropped 2lb; ought to fare better

runners.name: Scenario
runners.spotlight: Couple of valid excuses since a Bath win in the spring; there was a poor effort on Tapeta last time but she's capable of popping up back on turf.
runners.comment: Couple of valid excuses since a Bath win; there was a poor effort on Tapeta latest

runners.name: Lhebayeb
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last June (soft) off a 4lb higher mark; wants a good test over this trip but is capable of bouncing back, having been given an 11-week break since a couple of lesser efforts in Tapeta 0-50s.
runners.comment: Capable of bouncing back, having been given an 11-week break since a couple of lesser runs

runners.name: Zambezi Diamond
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden who missed a chunk of her career; has not always looked straightforward, hanging badly left throughout last time (7f) having made most of the running; has the option of going far side from stall 3 and it could well be all or nothing.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; has not always looked straightforward; could be all or nothing

runners.name: Dinah Myte
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent filly who popped up at a big price in a busy finish on Tapeta a fortnight ago; there can't be any complaints about a 2lb rise but history suggests she's no certainty to repeat the dose.
runners.comment: No complaints about a 2lb rise but history suggests she's no certainty to repeat the dose

runners.name: Redhot Whisper
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo but hard to place since and remains a maiden; found his level again of late and would be suited by ground softer than good, having been inclined to lug left round Bath the last twice; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Found his level again of late; would be suited by ground softer than good; major player

runners.name: Miss Chester
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent filly whose best efforts have come on Polytrack; achieved little in her three runs on turf, latterly when beaten a long way at Newbury a month ago; it's hard to know what to expect in truth but the temptation is to search elsewhere.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent filly; best efforts have come on Polytrack; hard to know what's coming

runners.name: Sub Thirteen
runners.spotlight: Has done most racing, and gained all his wins, in sprints, apparently not seeing it out in a few runs over 7f; having a first run in a 0-55 but has regressed this year and stamina would have to be a concern.
runners.comment: Most racing, and all his wins, in sprints; stamina would have to be a concern

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: Patchy round Bath for his current yard this year, mainly over further; ran poorly ten days ago, hard to predict and some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Patchy round Bath for current yard this year, mainly over further; ran poorly ten days ago

races.race_name: opx.io Operating System For Continuous Improvement Handicap (Chepstow Mile Series Qual') (Div II)

runners.name: Desert Charm
runners.spotlight: Lost her way last year after a Yarmouth win (good) off 2lb lower; fair comeback on Tapeta last month was more like it, when staying on over 7f, and she holds decent claims (taken out recently on account of soft ground).
runners.comment: Fair comeback on Tapeta last month was more like it (7f) and she holds decent claims

runners.name: Racing Demon
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a second in four starts over C&D and he is running at this lower level for just the second time, so there are positives; on the flip side, he's almost certain to miss the break and can give it away completely at the stalls; risky.
runners.comment: More than capable at this level but can give it away completely at the stalls; risky

runners.name: Homeland
runners.spotlight: Patchy for Michael Dods last year before being sold for 4,000gns; stays further and wants a good test at this trip; solid comeback from six months off over C&D (soft) nine days ago and he is entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Solid comeback from six months off over C&D (soft) nine days ago; entitled to improve

runners.name: Rival
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2022 and nothing like as good now; entitled to have needed his C&D comeback at his age nine days ago after a lengthy layoff but finished well held.
runners.comment: Entitled to have needed his C&D comeback nine days ago but finished well held

runners.name: A Rose Adaay
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden whose best efforts have come here, latterly on her first run at beyond sprint trips over C&D behind Scarfo nine days ago (both well drawn); a 3lb swing entitles her to at least get closer.
runners.comment: Third to Scarfo over C&D latest (both well drawn); 3lb swing entitles her to get closer

runners.name: Seconds Count
runners.spotlight: Limited maiden for William Haggas and changed hands for 6,000gns last autumn; lugged right more than once but offered a bit more in the hood back from a layoff last time (AW) and Rossa Ryan is a positive booking.
runners.comment: Bit more in a hood back from a layoff last time (AW) and Rossa Ryan is a positive booking

runners.name: Haaf A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent mare who's won only once; solid comeback effort in first-time cheekpieces but not as good back on the AW last time (taken out since on account of unsuitable good ground); others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Inconsistent mare who's won only once; backward step last time; others are more convincing
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Hammal
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for three runs for his current yard, including when well held on his handicap debut last time; little to recommend him.
runners.comment: Triple-figure prices for three runs for his current yard; little to recommend him

runners.name: Scarfo
runners.spotlight: Made the most of his rail draw (he's landed the top stall again today) when winning under Zoe Lewis (rides Homeland today) over C&D on soft ground nine days ago; this race looks a bit deeper but he's only 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Well drawn over C&D (soft) nine days ago; this looks a bit deeper but he's only 2lb higher

runners.name: Diamondsinthesand
runners.spotlight: Raced exclusively in sprints for the last year or so and has been this far only twice; doesn't arrive in much form and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Raced this far only twice; doesn't arrive in much form; others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Praise Indeed
runners.spotlight: Three modest efforts for Tom Ward last summer; this race is more realistic for her new yard now she's handicapping back from 11 months off, but market strength would be a helpful guide; taken out over C&D recently on account of soft ground.
runners.comment: This handicap debut is more realistic for new yard; market strength would be helpful guide
runners.owner: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud

races.race_name: William Hill Royal Ascot Epic Boosts EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wise And Wonderful
runners.spotlight: Outpaced in the straight but plugged on for a respectable fourth (of five) behind a better-fancied stablemate at Kempton 19 days ago (6f); plenty of stamina in her pedigree and 7f should suit; capable of much better in time.
runners.comment: Outpaced over 6f on Kempton debut but it was still a fair start; capable of much better

runners.name: March Lilly
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 7f/1m winner (RPR 89), herself a half-sister to 1m3f Flat and 2m3f hurdle/2m4f chase winner Line Of Descent, out of 1m winning half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Albabilia; stable enjoying a good year with 2yos; betting to guide.
runners.comment: First foal of a 7f/1m winner (RPR 89); stable having a good season with 2yos; check market
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Likira Rose
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Stormy Girl (6f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 104), Dirty Rascal (6f/7f including 2yo/AW; 103), The Outlaw (1m; 83) and Silent Strike (6f AW; 81); dam 7f AW winner (71); stable's record with 2yos doesn't suggest this will be her day.
runners.comment: 40,000gns half-sister to four winners; yard not known for debutante scorers

runners.name: Loveulongtime
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Ardaddy (7f AW; RPR 64) and Little Venice (1m2f Flat, 62; 1m7f hurdle); dam 7f-9.4f winner (71), out of German 1m3f winning half-sister to German 1m3f Listed winner Lagalp; a rare 2yo runner for the stable.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners out of a minor winner; a rare 2yo runner for the stable

runners.name: Northern Viola
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 1m AW winner Northern Cracksman (RPR 68); dam 6f winner (64); stable having a quiet spell and still to get off the mark with 2yos this year.
runners.comment: Half-sister to 1m AW winner Northern Cracksman; stable still seeking first 2yo win of 2026

runners.name: Starlight Mirage
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to winners Sisyphean (1m including AW; RPR 101), The Watcher (6f 2yo/7f AW; 87) and Espionne (7f 2yo; 80); unraced dam closely related to useful 7f 2yo/2m hurdle winner Blue Lemons, out of close relative of 1m4f Group 2 winner Time On; she represents a powerful stable and makes plenty of appeal.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners, notably Sisyphean (RPR 101); powerful yard; newcomer of note
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Aura Of Melania
runners.spotlight: 28-1, green and well beaten in a 6f fillies' novice at Ripon 18 days ago; the extra furlong isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: 28-1 when well beaten on last month's Ripon debut (6f); big step forward required

races.race_name: William Hill Epic Boost 8am Tomorrow Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Machadadorp
runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut effort when winning Chester maiden last month (7f, good); not ideally drawn, but a chance
runners.comment: Improved on debut when winning Chester maiden last time; further progress likely; chance

runners.name: Crossbar

runners.name: Twin And Tonic

runners.comment: By Century Dream; thirteenth foal of a 7f/1m4f winner; best watched on debut

runners.name: Rogue Invincible

runners.comment: Needs to improve on his two runs in 7f/1m novices last season

races.race_name: Heineken 0.0 Handicap

runners.name: Supreme Dancer

runners.name: Thestral

runners.name: Sports Day

runners.name: Thornaby Annie

runners.comment: Maiden who has been a close second in two 7f Redcar handicaps on last two starts; chance

runners.name: Inferno

runners.comment: Each-way chance on his Nottingham fifth last month; excuses for poor run on AW latest

runners.name: Hey Havana
runners.spotlight: Ex-Nigel Tinkler; close second to a former stable companion at Redcar on stable/seasonal debut (7f, good); that form hasn't worked out particularly well but should improve for the race; well drawn but stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-Nigel Tinkler; good 2nd on seasonal/stable debut at Redcar; should improve for the run

runners.name: Space Moon
runners.spotlight: Maiden; fair third at Nottingham last time (8.5f, good) but stall 11 a negative.
runners.comment: Maiden; fair third at Nottingham last time (8.5f, good) but stall 11 a negative

runners.name: Balmoral Boy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed, but well beaten on his handicap debut (1m, good to soft) last time, so needs to improve.
runners.comment: Unexposed; well beaten on handicap debut and improvement needed

runners.name: Sailor Batt
runners.spotlight: Yet to win in ten runs, but placed on five occasions, including last time when third at Ripon (1m, good to soft); back after a short break; poorly drawn.
runners.comment: 0-10; placed on five occasions (third last time); best form on softish ground; poor draw

runners.name: Celtic Chariot
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in a Doncaster novice on debut (7f, good to firm; winner and fourth have won since); hasn't done quite as well since; quite interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Good third at Doncaster on debut; not fared as well since; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Deep Sleep
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; has shaped creditably when fourth on both runs in handicaps (1m, 8.5f); interesting with cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Fourth on both starts since going into handicaps; interesting with cheekpieces tried

runners.name: Horwich
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on at Wolverhampton (7f); well beaten at Leicester on only run on turf; bit to prove.

runners.name: Howsham
runners.spotlight: Modest form so far, and now tried in a visor; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Modest form so far, and now tried in a visor; plenty to prove

races.race_name: Amstel Apprentice Handicap (Go Racing In Yorkshire Future Stars Apprentice Series)

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: Tough front-runner; ran a rare poor race last time but had been in good form prior to that, notably when a close third at Ayr last month (1m, good to firm); major chance.
runners.comment: Tough front-runner; below par last time but in decent form before that; major chance

runners.name: Popeye Doyle

runners.comment: Two AW wins for Hugo Palmer last term but below-par for new yard; change of headgear

runners.name: Roaring Ralph
runners.spotlight: In tremendous form this year, and has won four of his last five races, three over 1m at Southwell and last time over 7f at Ayr (good to firm); has gone up 19lb (up 3lb for Ayr win) and should go well again.
runners.comment: Has won four of his last five races, latest at Ayr; up 19lb; should go well again

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Seemed suited by a change of headgear for he has been in the frame in all four starts since cheekpieces went on (wore a visor previously), last time finishing third at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm); each-way chance
runners.comment: Run well all four starts with cheekpieces, last time a 1l third Beverley; each-way chance

runners.name: Sunny Orange
runners.spotlight: Four of his five wins have been at Beverley, the latest in April (8.5f, good to firm); has also won on good to soft, but not been at his best recently
runners.comment: Four of five wins have been at Beverley, latest in April; below his best recently

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: 0-13 on turf, but has won three races on the AW, the latest at Newcastle in January (1m); not been at his best recently.
runners.comment: Three AW wins, latest in January, but 0-13 on turf; not been in top form recently

runners.name: Great Blasket
runners.spotlight: Has won seven of his 24 races on turf and sound run last time when 0.75l third at Nottingham (8.5f, good); on a good mark at present and could go well.
runners.comment: 7-24 on turf; sound run when close third at Nottingham; should give another good account

races.race_name: William Hill Extra Places Every Day Handicap (Div I)

runners.comment: Ex-Adrian Wintle; good 2nd at Chepstow on reappearance; well drawn; chance on stable debut

runners.name: Desert Master

runners.name: Isla Bella

runners.comment: Won a 6f Brighton maiden in 2024; not won since and only fifth at Pontefract last Monday

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: Poor 16-race maiden and makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Poor 16-race maiden and makes little appeal

runners.name: Blackjack

runners.comment: Has won on turf, but better on AW; probably best watched after a break having had wind op

runners.name: Bayraat

runners.comment: 1-32; two good seconds over 6f at Redcar this term; well drawn; one with a chance

runners.name: Amerjeet
runners.spotlight: Yet to win on turf and needs to improve on his sixth over C&D last time (good to firm).
runners.comment: Yet to win on turf and needs to improve on last time's C&D sixth

runners.name: Solar Biricz

runners.name: Ramon Di Loria

runners.comment: Dual AW winner this year; ran okay on recent turf return; this stiffer track suits him

runners.name: Rain Cap

runners.comment: Placed twice on Tapeta this spring and will be suited by return to turf; in the mix

runners.name: Baby Rover

runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Wait For It

runners.comment: Disappointing filly who suffered heavy defeat over 1m on Tapeta on latest start in January

runners.name: Rambuso Creek

runners.comment: Ended long losing run in Nottingham classified on Thursday; this is tougher under penalty

runners.name: Invincible Ruby

runners.comment: Took big step back in right direction when keeping-on fourth at Hamilton last month

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Live Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Maynora
runners.spotlight: Came good at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) in April, his 11th start, and had awkward draw on the wing when fourth off new mark at Ripon (6f, good) last month; remains very much of interest.
runners.comment: Off the mark two starts ago and had awkward draw when fourth last time; a player

runners.spotlight: Scored over 5f on Tapeta in November but her subsequent AW form was underwhelming and, following a break, she was soundly beaten when back on turf last month; needs headgear switch to give her a good boost.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when back on turf last month; needs boost from headgear switch

runners.name: Howzak

runners.name: Belling The Cat
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but has performed well in all four handicaps, most recently when fourth off 11 in first-time cheekpieces at Catterick (7f, good) last month; may benefit from this drop back in trip; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: 0-7 but has performed well in all four handicaps; probably won't be far away

runners.name: Great Success
runners.spotlight: Showed promise during three-race 2yo campaign but has struggled in both handicaps this term (7f/6f) and needs to get his career back on track.
runners.comment: Showed promise as a 2yo but has struggled in both handicaps this season

runners.name: April Diamond
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three qualifying races (5f-6f) and is not obviously well treated for this handicap debut, but she's open to improvement and worth keeping an eye on in the betting.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated for handicap debut but worth keeping an eye on in the betting

runners.name: Arctic Fox
runners.spotlight: Placed at Catterick (1m6f/2m, good) on both appearances this season; back up in grade today but won this race off today's mark a year ago (soft ground); shortlisted.
runners.comment: Won this race off today's mark a year ago and placed on both outings this season

runners.name: Stand Strong
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging seasonal debut at Catterick (2m, good; behind Arctic Fox) last month but his only win from 18 starts came on Tapeta and others have more pressing claims; refitted blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging seasonal debut in May but only career win came on Tapeta
runners.owner: A Whillans, C Spark, J Wright

runners.name: Clansman
runners.spotlight: Dual Tapeta winner this year (1m4f/2m); also effective on turf but would appeal much more on soft/heavy ground and probably doesn't have much margin for error off this mark.
runners.comment: Dual Tapeta winner this year; best turf form is on soft/heavy ground; others preferred
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Gentle Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this sort of trip in September but was not quite in same form in two four-runner races last month and needs to up his game.
runners.comment: This trip suits him but he wasn't quite at his best in two four-runner races last month
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Marbuzet
runners.spotlight: Won twice in blinkers last season (1m7f/2m); back on a workable mark but made a low-key reappearance in cheekpieces (retained) last month and needs that run to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Won twice last season but made a low-key reappearance last month

runners.name: Trojan Sun
runners.spotlight: Won four times last season (1m6f-2m2f) and looked as good as ever when clear third in quite valuable Musselburgh handicap (2m, good) in April but didn't fire at Hamilton last month and has now disappointed on four of his last five appearances.
runners.comment: Good third at Musselburgh in April but has disappointing on four of last five starts

runners.name: Banderas
runners.spotlight: Not seen on the Flat since hard-fought course win (2m1f, soft) last summer but produced career-best hurdle performance when winning on good ground last month and is evidently in very good nick; respected.
runners.comment: Not seen on Flat since course win last July but in excellent form over hurdles last month

runners.name: Scotland The Brave
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark on Tapeta (2m) in early 2025 but returned from long absence with poor performance here (1m3f) on Tuesday and has a lot more to prove than some of these.
runners.comment: Returned from long absence when poor performance here (1m3f) on Tuesday

runners.name: Dance Time
runners.spotlight: 6-19 on AW but 0-11 on turf; comes here after two down-the-field runs in blinkers (now removed) at Chester last month (1m4f/2m); not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Twice down the field in blinkers at Chester last month and now 0-11 on turf

runners.name: Ouro Preto
runners.spotlight: 3-15 when trained in France, the latest win in a slow-ground claimer last September; finished last in handicaps on both starts for Iain Jardine last month (1m3f/2m); very difficult to enthuse over at present.
runners.comment: Ex-French 5yo; finished last on first two British starts (1m3f/2m)

runners.name: Torcello
runners.spotlight: 12yo who was reportedly well suited by the soft ground when suddenly bouncing back to form with 6l win at Haydock (2m; refitted blinkers) last month; subsequent 5lb rise is not excessive but drying conditions may scupper this follow-up attempt.
runners.comment: Won by 6l on soft ground last month; drying conditions not in his favour here

runners.name: Pyramid Place
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler in his prime but has been out of form over jumps for a good while now, including when last of six on recent stable debut; this will be his first Flat run since a heavy defeat in summer 2023.
runners.comment: Out of form for a good while over jumps; first Flat run since summer 2023

races.race_name: Follow Racing TV On X Handicap

runners.name: Beaming Light
runners.spotlight: Handicapped to win and although his form-figures are uninspiring, there have been some mitigating factors; the race wasn't run to suit him at Wolverhampton two weeks ago and he's worth another chance.
runners.comment: Handicapped to win and wouldn't judge him too harshly on latest AW run; respected

runners.name: Bellasio
runners.spotlight: C&D win 12 months ago and better form when second on his next two starts; not quite hit those heights this year but returning to this track may help.
runners.comment: C&D winner; not found his best form this year but return to Carlisle can help

runners.name: Age Of Time
runners.spotlight: Both wins on AW and his turf form leaves him with more to prove; can build on recent stable debut but others look stronger for win purposes.
runners.comment: Two AW wins but his turf form isn't so convincing; others stronger for win purposes

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner at up to 7f; finished his race off on the front foot here last week (7f, soft) and no doubt he can win races off this mark; slight stamina query but not ruled out.
runners.comment: Finished off well over 7f here last week; good mark but still to prove he needs this far

runners.name: Runninsonofagun
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate but he ended 2025 in good form and he ran his best race of this campaign when third over 1m1f here last week; runs off the same mark and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Infrequent winner but on a good mark and returned to form when third here last week (1m1f)

runners.name: Hostility
runners.spotlight: Exposed 17-race maiden but he has dropped a long way in the weights and he ran well when second over 1m1f here 17 days ago; others still appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Exposed 17-race maiden; good 2nd here (1m1f) last month but remains vulnerable for the win

runners.name: Betty Bassett
runners.spotlight: Chance on her best form for Ian Williams but she was tailed off on her seasonal/stable debut last month; questions to answer despite dropping back into a Class 6.
runners.comment: 80-1 and tailed off on last month's stable/seasonal debut; something to prove

runners.name: Between Me And U
runners.spotlight: Perhaps needed the run when sixth on his return from nine months off in May; dropped 3lb and not discounted for a trainer who also runs Soames Forsyte.
runners.comment: Sharper for last month's comeback and lurks on a menacing mark; trainer runs two

runners.name: Soames Forsyte
runners.spotlight: Popped up at Southwell (1m) off a 2lb higher mark in January; the odd good run since but he needs to bounce back from a lacklustre AW run last month.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but he needs to bounce back from a lacklustre AW run last month

runners.name: Jewel Maker
runners.spotlight: Veteran who ran his best race of the campaign when fourth over 1m1f here last month; should run his race again but might have to make do with some minor money.
runners.comment: Veteran who ran his best race of the year when 4th here last month (1m1f); not discounted

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than for this season's two wins, the latest a last-gasp success at Leicester last month (1m, good to firm); unplaced twice since; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Two wins this year off a 1lb lower mark; unplaced on last two runs; others stronger

runners.name: Gainsbourg
runners.spotlight: Of interest on 2024 form; two heavy defeats last summer and now returns from 344 days off; cheekpieces back on and he needs a market check.
runners.comment: Two heavy defeats in 2025 and absent 344 days; on good mark though and needs market check

races.race_name: Racing TV Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Velvet Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Launched handicap career with 7f wins at Newmarket (good to firm) and Catterick (good) last month; weakened into fourth of six after making the running at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) last time but further progress remains possible.
runners.comment: Won first two handicaps; managed only fourth last time but further progress still possible
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Douwantitrightnow
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only five runs but was soundly beaten in both handicaps this year (7f/6f) and needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only five runs but was soundly beaten in first two handicaps

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D (soft) just under a year ago and has been in good form lately; close second off today's mark here (7.7f, soft) a week ago and also suited by good ground; likely contender again.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last summer and went close over 7.7f here last week; likely contender

runners.name: Amelia's Joy
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f AW winner since fitted with today's headgear combination in March and both recent turf runs were perfectly respectable; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner this year and both recent turf runs were perfectly respectable

runners.name: Sahara Magic
runners.spotlight: Made low-key handicap debut on AW in January and was unable to land a significant blow when sixth of eight on last month's turf debut at Catterick (7f, good); improvement required.
runners.comment: Unable to land a significant blow on recent turf debut and now 0-5; improvement required

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when tried in cheekpieces (removed here) ten days ago and now 0-8 but posted three good efforts beforehand (6f-7f) and will be dangerous off this mark if quickly back on song.
runners.comment: Shade disappointing on latest outing but in good form beforehand; player if back on song

runners.name: Anorah Unleashed
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; kept on well for third on seasonal debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in early April but two lesser efforts have followed; back up in trip here; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f on seasonal debut but two lesser efforts have followed; back up in trip.

runners.name: Albeseeingyer
runners.spotlight: Without a win since summer 2024 but her second at Leicester (7f, good) in April was a good effort and she didn't run badly when sixth from awkward draw at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) this month; 2-3 at this track (both wins over C&D in 2022) and may well be the answer.
runners.comment: Hasn't won for a while but her Leicester second in April was a good effort; this C&D suits

runners.name: Pickersgill
runners.spotlight: Came good for Iain Jardine at fourth attempt, with clearcut 7f AW win in April; disappointed when back on turf this month (6f) but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Disappointed on recent turf return but has claims if judged on April's AW win

runners.name: Sailor's Return
runners.spotlight: 0-5 when based in France but made the frame in two AW handicaps at Chantilly (7f/1m) this year; makes British debut after three-month break; market helpful.
runners.comment: 0-5 in France but ran well on last two outings; market helpful on British debut

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Saisons d'Or
runners.spotlight: Veteran who gamely made all at Southwell (7f, AW) in April, his 12th win; only 1lb higher here and did pretty well to finish fifth at Catterick (7f, good) last month, given he pulled hard in the early stages (not a regular trait) and was denied a clear run; considered.
runners.comment: Veteran; only 1lb higher than for AW win in April and ran okay on turf last time; possible

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 7yo who battled well over C&D (good) last month and has now won four times this year; might be a shade vulnerable after latest 4lb rise but ought to give another good account.
runners.comment: Resurgent 7yo who battled well over C&D last month and has now won four times this year

runners.name: Jamaican Storm
runners.spotlight: Just did enough to open account at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March, his 17th start, and also ran well when third behind The Gay Blade over C&D (good) last month, having made a big forward move in the early stages; his tendency to race too freely remains a worry but he can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Ran well when third to The Gay Blade over C&D last month; can make presence felt again

runners.name: King Sharja
runners.spotlight: Gained all three turf wins at Beverley but ran well in defeat over C&D (good; other division of race won by The Gay Blade) 17 days ago; still on workable mark after 2lb rise; must be considered.
runners.comment: Second over C&D last month and still on workable mark after 2lb rise

runners.name: Lightning Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who ended last season with a disappointing effort (1m) and has finished down the field on both appearances this term (1m/6f); needs first-time tongue-tie to make a difference.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who disappointed on first two starts this season; tongue-tie now added

runners.name: Good Karma
runners.spotlight: Consistent on AW this year and was nudged up only 2lb for his win in refitted cheekpieces at Wolverhampton (7f) a fortnight ago; also suited by turf; respected.
runners.comment: Consistent on AW this year; nudged up only 2lb for recent wins; turf also suits; respected

runners.name: Lesley's Boy
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old but bettered his poor seasonal debut when fifth of 12 at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) a fortnight ago; continues to lurk on a dangerous mark and is well worth another crack at this sort of trip; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Ran okay on AW a fortnight ago and continues to lurk on a dangerous mark

runners.name: El Pinto
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; put some disappointing efforts behind him and made bold bid from the front before weakening into fourth behind The Gay Blade over C&D (good) last month but others appeal more all the same.
runners.comment: Ran quite well from the front when fourth over C&D last month but is now 0-8

races.race_name: T. Murray & Son Ltd. Beginners Chase

runners.name: Le Coq Hardi
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins came on good in 2024 and he's 0-7 since switching to chasing but has been placed five times, best effort coming over this course and distance a year ago when second in competitive handicap off 133; bit disappointing as odds-on fav when beaten 12l 25 days ago but should come on for that and has an experience edge.
runners.comment: 0-7 in chases, some good efforts; second at Limerick 25 days ago; rated 135, sets standard

runners.name: Impero
runners.spotlight: Very useful five-time winning hurdler who has put in two promising runs this year when second in competitive Killarney handicap (2m1f, gd) in May off 136 before running to a similar level when beaten 7l into third at Kelso 22 days ago; steps up in trip on chase debut and could be good enough to make a winning start.
runners.comment: Second in good h'cap off 136 last month; ran to form at Kelso; chase debut; solid claims

runners.name: Coyote Spirit
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner, coming in easy success in very testing ground at Navan in February; consistent in maidens and handicaps prior to to being tailed off at Cork 71 days ago; rated 123 over hurdles and should have place claims if transferring form to fences.
runners.comment: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; handles all ground; makes chase debut, bit to find

runners.name: Londonofficecallin
runners.spotlight: Verastile dual-purpose sort who's scored four times over hurdles and six on the Flat, including Cork Derby last month on soft; latest hurdle win at Punchestown (23.5f, good) a year ago off 115; ran just okay when well held fifth over fences 15 days ago here; needs a fair step forward on that.
runners.comment: Useful dual-purpose sort; below-par over fences 15 days ago; needs more

runners.name: Glen To Glen
runners.spotlight: Looked a nice prospect when landing a 3yo maiden hurdle at Cork in December; down the field when well-fancied in Fred Winter at Cheltenham; two poor Flat runs since and jumping let him down when fifth at Ballinrobe 21 days ago; should improve plenty for that but needs to up in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden h'dle winner; let down by jumping on chase debut; step forward likely but needs to

runners.name: Following Orders
runners.spotlight: Some fair placed form in maiden hurdles last year on good, most notably when runner-up to Power Of Words at Roscommon (2m4f); no impact on chase debut here last month and up against it.
runners.comment: Some fair placed form in maiden hurdles last year; no impact on chase debut last month

runners.name: Carrigane
runners.spotlight: Five point-to-point starts; best effort when beaten a length at Ballindenisk 29 days ago; makes rules debut in tough race.
runners.comment: Placed twice in four point-to-points; makes rules debut in tough race

runners.name: Road To The Weir
runners.spotlight: Lowly-rated over fences and hurdles; unlikely to be making an impact here
runners.comment: Lowly-rated over fences and hurdles; unlikely to be making an impact here

runners.name: Ephesus
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat and a maiden hurdle winner at Limerick (2m1f, gd) last year; finished last season on a low note with a pair of underwhelming runs on the Flat and over hurdles; makes chase debut after 168 days off.
runners.comment: Dual Flat and a maiden hurdle winner; below-form in the winter; chase debut after a break

runners.name: Finnfaraway
runners.spotlight: No impact in five starts under rules; pulled up in last three point-to-points; no reason to fancy.
runners.comment: No impact in five starts under rules; pulled up in last three point-to-points

runners.name: Orkneys Jack
runners.spotlight: No impact in three maiden hurdles last year; off the mark on ninth attempt in points last month; tough assignment on chase debut under rules.
runners.comment: Won point-to-point on ninth attempt last month; tough ask on chase debut under rules

runners.name: Positive Energy
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner at Naas last year (2m, heavy); held form on the Flat during the summer but has been below form in last four outings over hurdles and fences; others preferred.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle and Flat winner; not shown enough in two chase starts to be winning this

races.race_name: Mongey Communications Galway Plate Trial Handicap Chase

runners.name: Gaelic Arc
runners.spotlight: Back to form with third chase win in fine style at Punchestown nine days ago (20.5f, yielding), making all to win by 3.25l; 5lb rise for that, but won this race off this mark last year; obvious chance if in the same form but consistency not a strong suit.
runners.comment: Back to form on latest, won this off the same mark last year, consistency not strong suit
runners.owner: Mark R D Fleming / T Westropp Bennett

runners.name: El Champo
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins last year included a valuable handicap at Killarney (2m6f, gd-yld); runs off the same mark now but hasn't looked a winner in waiting on recent starts (latest fourth a remote one); needs to rediscover best.
runners.comment: Runs off last winning mark but out of form so far this year, needs to rediscover best

runners.name: Warriors Wood
runners.spotlight: Made it third time lucky over fences when bolting up over C&D last month; 6lb rise for that 7l win seems reasonable and while this is more demanding, he's unexposed in this sphere and there could still be more to come.
runners.comment: Off the mark over fences over C&D latest, up 6lb, this harder but could be more to come

runners.name: Boston Rover
runners.spotlight: Winning sequence came to an abrupt halt when pulled up at Limerick in the Munster National when last seen in October (soft ground put forward as excuse); capable of better than that but wouldn't want to see much rain.
runners.comment: Won three before pulled up in Munster National, capable of better, wants good ground

runners.name: Milan Forth
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb lower at Cork in January (2m4f, yld-sft); excellent effort off 3lb lower over C&D on latest when a 0.5l second behind Littlefoot; this race is deeper so more needed to win but place claims again at the very least.
runners.comment: Excellent run off 3lb lower over C&D latest, this harder but place claims again at least

runners.name: Wonleg
runners.spotlight: Has run well but has ultimately come up short in three runs since winning at Wexford last July (2m, good); far from disgraced but still beaten 18l on latest in premier handicap at Aintree (2m, gd-sft) and same mark now; longer trip needs to eke out more (good chance will stay on breeding).
runners.comment: Beaten 18l off this mark at Aintree latest (2m), longer trip needs to eke out more

runners.name: Marv Michael
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins for Henry de Bromhead, both over this C&D; is 4lb higher than when winning here in May 2025; third in this off 7lb higher last year; soundly beaten on debut for this yard last month but may have needed that; blinkers replace cheekpieces and rider takes off handy 3lb.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, third in this last year, may have needed latest, new headgear combo

runners.name: A Wave Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: 5-46 over fences, including three over C&D; is 1lb lower than when scoring here in September and took a step in the right direction when fifth over C&D on latest (behind Milan Forth); can't be discounted given his affinity for this venue.
runners.comment: 5-46 over fences, three C&D wins, step in right direction latest, can't be discounted

runners.name: Indiana Jones
runners.spotlight: Seemed to be coming back to form with successive fifths in good handicaps at Aintree (2m5f, gd-sft) and Punchestown (16.5f, yielding) but failed to fire back at Punchestown behind Gaelic Arc latest (2m, good); hasn't won since March 2023 and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Disappointed latest after two decent fifths, hasn't won since March 2023, others preferred

runners.name: Ol Man Dingle
runners.spotlight: Won first two chase starts in the autumn including a Grade 3 novice at Cork (2m4f, gd-yld); below best next three starts but much more like it when a 10.5l third in the Mayo National last time (25.5f, good), not quite getting home; should go well again down in trip.
runners.comment: Back on song in Mayo National last time, shorter trip here a plus, go well again

runners.name: Riaan
runners.spotlight: Mark of 136 is tempting on the form of his 3.75l defeat of the 146 rated Jesse Evans at Sligo in October; strip fitter for remote fourth in Punchestown Grade 3 novice on latest; interesting with rider taking off a handy 5lb.
runners.comment: Looks potentially well treated on win at Sligo in October, sharper for latest, interesting

runners.name: Rising Dust
runners.spotlight: Rattled in a hat-trick at Thurles at 2m5f-25.5f last winter, going up a cumulative 41lb; came up short off revised mark at Kempton (3m, gd-sft) when last seen in February; needs to resume progress after break.
runners.comment: Went up 41lb for Thurles hat-trick, came up short last time at Kempton, rain a negative

runners.name: Linden Arden
runners.spotlight: 2-7 over fences; remains 5lb above last win in this sphere at Limerick (19.5f, yld-sft) in March 2025; below best last three starts over hurdles and fences on soft-heavy, could bounce back returned to better ground.
runners.comment: 2-7 over fences, poor on testing ground recently, could rebound on sounder surface

runners.name: Drumgill
runners.spotlight: Very capable performer on his day over hurdles and fences; 3-19 in this sphere; soundly beaten in Mayo National latest but this shorter trip should be more his bag and is just 3lb above last win; not discounted but lots of rain would be a worry.
runners.comment: 3-19 over fences, just 3lb above last win, trip stretched latest, rain would be a worry

races.race_name: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Now Mares Hurdle

runners.name: Poetic Twist
runners.spotlight: Runner up in first two starts over hurdles; off the mark in a mares' maiden hurdle at Tramore on latest (21.5f, good); debatable whether this drop in trip will suit but she's fit and in form and should be in the mix again.
runners.comment: 21.5f maiden hurdle win at Tramore latest, drop in trip a query but in form, in the mix

runners.name: Kenisa Sport
runners.spotlight: Downpatrick bumper (2m2f, good) and course maiden hurdle winner (2m, good) was turned over at short odds in a Perth mares' novice when last seen in September; rated 130 but lacks a recent run; market can guide.
runners.comment: Course maiden hurdle winner, beaten fav' at Perth when last seen, check market

runners.name: Maximillian Lady
runners.spotlight: Won a Galway bumper (16.5f, gd-yld) in good style last September; followed that up with a 2l second in a Listed bumper at Gowran (16.5f, soft); never involved on debut for this yard in Grade 3 bumper after a break at Punchestown; not discounted on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, disappointed on stable debut latest, not discounted on hurdles' bow

runners.name: Venusienne
runners.spotlight: Placed in a listed mares' hurdle in France in 2023; out of depth in graded races first two runs for this yard in March/April last year; justified short odds dropped to maiden company at Tramore (20.5, yielding) when last seen in October; big chance if in the same form now.
runners.comment: Justified short odds at Tramore in October, off since, big chance if in same form now

runners.name: Love At Sea
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Cork last August (held by Kenisa Sport on a line through the runner up Kabylia on that form); not disgraced in Listowel handicap hurdle when last seen in September; has gone well fresh; place claims if fit.
runners.comment: Cork maiden hurdle winner, off since ok handicap run last Sept', place chance if fit

runners.name: Where Eagles Fly
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in both point-to-points; watching brief advised on Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in both starts in points, watching brief advised on Rules' debut

runners.name: Carmel's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly; talented on the Flat, rated 86; won a 1m4f Cork maiden (sft-hvy) last month; placed in a Bellewstown maiden on good ground last year so conditions fine and she's interesting on hurdles' debut.
runners.comment: Rated 86 on the Flat, 1m4f Cork maiden win latest, interesting on hurdling debut

runners.name: Mila Ballerina
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a Punchestown bumper last month when 40-1; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Punchestown bumper last month, seems safe to rule out

runners.name: Talas Kera
runners.spotlight: No signs of winning potential in five bumpers and two maiden hurdles; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No signs of winning potential in bumpers or maiden hurdles, hard to fancy

runners.name: Another Blunder
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in all four starts; hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in all four starts, hard to make a case for

runners.name: Maybe Someday
runners.spotlight: Mild promise in a Tramore bumper but three maiden hurdle runs not encouraging; off 268 days and tongue tie and cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Poor in all three runs over hurdles, off 268 days, new headgear combination tried now

races.race_name: Old Harbour Bar Tullamore (Q.R.) Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Brohatna
runners.spotlight: Two fair efforts in maiden hurdles at Downpatrick on good after low-key debut; ran well for a long way before fading beaten 13.5l into fifth behind Mr Master Mac over 2m3f 24 days ago; keen that day and hood goes on for drop in trip; form good enough to be involved here and he's been improving with each run.
runners.comment: Two fair efforts in maidens over further; drops in trip; form could be good enough here

runners.name: Silver Salute
runners.spotlight: Standout run came on debut in December when beaten 16l into seventh in decent maiden hurdle at Leopardstown; hasn't reproduced anything close to that in four runs since but return to good ground could suit and he'd be a danger if so.
runners.comment: Standout run on debut when seventh at Leop; not built on that; 107 days off; a danger

runners.name: Sharu
runners.spotlight: Improved slightly on two moderate bumper runs when beaten 12.5l into sixth at Tramore 16 days ago; drop in trip should be fine and he's in good hands; likely improver.
runners.comment: Improved on bumper runs, sixth on hurdles debut; down in trip, can improve; in good hands

runners.name: Silly Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in bumpers but was never sighted on debut over hurdles at Wexford before stepping forward markedly when beaten 8.5l into fifth at Clonmel (19.5f, gd); travelled well that day so this drop in trip should suit and he has a big chance if building on that.
runners.comment: Improved markedly with fair run at Clonmel lto; that puts him in with claims down in trip

runners.name: Great Entirely
runners.spotlight: Reasonable debut when sixth in maiden hurdle at Wexford (2m4f, gd) but was never involved before being pulled up before two out 18 days ago at Limerick; tongue-strap goes on now.
runners.comment: Reasonable debut at Wexford before PU at Limerick; chance on debut run

runners.name: Sky Lewis
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; struggled in two bumpers almost three years apart and a maiden hurdle 33 days ago; needs big step forward.
runners.comment: Second reserve; struggled in two bumpers and maiden hurdle; needs big step forward

runners.name: Rose Mountain
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; nothing to recommend her on based on three moderate runs.
runners.comment: Third reserve; nothing to recommend her on based on three very moderate runs

runners.name: Yolanda's Star
runners.spotlight: Improved a bit on poor debut at Downpatrick when beaten 22l at Punchestown (2m6f, gd) eight days ago; needs a big step forward here however.
runners.comment: Made moderate late gains on second hurdle start lto; needs to be sharper down in trip

runners.name: Jessie Roe
runners.spotlight: Moderate bumper form and pulled up before two out on hurdles debut 18 days ago; needs far more down in trip.
runners.comment: Moderate bumper form and PU on hurdles debut lto; needs far more down in trip

runners.name: Adifferentballowax
runners.spotlight: First reserve; improved on moderate debut when ninth at Limerick 18 days ago but still made little impression; in good hands though and outside claims.
runners.comment: First reserve; improved on moderate debut when ninth at Lim' lto; outside place claims
runners.owner: John F Doyle & R Bannerman & Mrs Sheila O'Ryan

runners.name: Scotty Joe
runners.spotlight: Some late progress when sixth on debut at Kilbeggan (2m2f, gd) on debut but step backwards at Punchestown nine days ago; needs more but not out of it.
runners.comment: Some late progress on debut, never involved lto over 2m; needs more but not out of it

runners.name: Arizona Robbins
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat, similar level of form in three hurdles starts; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Modest on the Flat, similar level of form in three hurdles starts; hard to recommend

runners.name: Urinmydreams
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a bumper and three maiden hurdles; needs a significant step forward.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in four starts; needs a big step forward

runners.name: Undertheinfluence
runners.spotlight: Not been disgraced in two hurdle starts but still beaten 39l at Punchestown nine days ago and is held by Brohatna on his Downpatrick run; needs more.
runners.comment: Not been disgraced in two hurdle starts but needs improvement and may be one for h'caps

runners.name: Drumadeise
runners.spotlight: Only ran to a very modest level on both starts to date; hard to see her finding the required improvement.
runners.comment: Well-held in two maiden hurdles and has plenty to find

runners.name: Betterluckthistime
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a pair of maiden hurdles, including at this track; probably needs handicaps.
runners.comment: Hard to see him finding the required improvement after making little impact in two starts

runners.name: Sonny Fella
runners.spotlight: Nothing to shout about in 14 hurdle starts and rated 72 as a result; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Not much to shout about in 16 starts; well held in h'cap off 72 lto; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Follow Kilbeggan On Instagram Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Jaadil
runners.spotlight: Proved progressive last year, scoring at Cork (18.5f, gd) in October either side of runner-up finishes; pulled up and fell early in two starts this year so needs to bounce back up in trip.
runners.comment: Progressive last year but PU and fell early last twice; up in trip now

runners.name: Ladies Well
runners.spotlight: Sole win in ten starts came off 2lb lower at Ballinrobe in August (2m6f, gd) before respectable eighth of 20 in 3m Down Royal handicap in October; remote second on chase debut at Kelso 22 days ago but could come on plenty for that and is a player.
runners.comment: H'cap winner last year; beaten a long way in h'cap chase lto; entitled to come on for that

runners.name: Queen Kulina
runners.spotlight: 0-17 but largely consistent in six starts in 2024 and 2025; was only beaten 1.5l into fourth off 4lb higher at Galway (3m, yld) in October; fell four out on return at Punchestown and weakened tamely at Clonmel last time; on a nice mark but needs more
runners.comment: Largely consistent last two years; weakened tamely lto; on nice mark, needs step forward

runners.name: Pilgrims Walk
runners.spotlight: No impact in three maiden hurdles; starts handicapping from fair mark and takes a big step up in trip.
runners.comment: No impact in three maiden hurdles; starts handicapping from fair mark up in trip

runners.name: Ballyredinkingston
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit in bumpers last year and was running okay at Clonmel 81 days ago before being brought down, albeit was on the retreat; well-treated based on early form if he can build on that up in trip.
runners.comment: Running well prior to being brought down at Clonmel lto; could go well if building on it

runners.name: Hay Baby
runners.spotlight: First reserve; hints of ability last year, finishing in the first five four times in a row in handicaps over shorter trips and well-backed on a few occasions; below-par run on return 36 days ago but entitled to come on for that and would be interesting if getting a run on stable debut for Emmet Mullins, particularly if the market spoke in her favour.
runners.comment: First reserve; hints of ability last year, below-par lto; of interest on stable debut

runners.name: Brianna Lily
runners.spotlight: First positive run in a while when beaten 5l into third at Clonmel (19.5f, gd) in May but failed to build on that when no impression at Tramore 17 days ago; step up in trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Positive run at Clonmel but well beaten at Tramore lto; tries a new trip

runners.name: Island McCoo
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 19 starts came in handicap chase at Tramore (2m6f, yld) last year; shaped well when staying-on fourth at Sligo last month and backed it up when beaten 2.5l into second by Mickey Hulie at Limerick 18 days ago; shapes as if step up in trip will suit and leading claims off 2lb higher with claimer taking off 7lb.
runners.comment: Shaped well when fourth at Sligo, backed that up second lto; strong claims up in trip

runners.name: Celtic Crossing
runners.spotlight: Entitled to need comeback run at Sligo after a long lay-off and ran just respectably when beaten 20l into fifth at Limerick 18 days ago; dropped 11lb in the weights since decent fifth at Roscommon in 2024 so could be well-treated but needs more up in trip.
runners.comment: Came on for reappearance with a fair run Limerick; extra trip should suit but needs more

runners.name: Roman Parish
runners.spotlight: Some decent placed form over fences last year, most notably when 6.5l second to Kyle Valley at Tramore (2m6f, yld) in handicap chase off 79 in May; proved he's in good heart when runner-up in point-to-point last month so he could figure.
runners.comment: Some decent placed runs over fences last year; second in point last month; could figure

runners.name: Tango Theatre
runners.spotlight: Sole success in 25 hurdle starts came when making all easily at Sligo (25.5f, gd) in August, won by 15l off 7lb lower; well-held in two starts since and returns from 234 days off; mark probably a bit high but 7lb claimer helps.
runners.comment: Made all at Sligo in Aug, well held in two starts after; needs to bounce back after break

runners.name: Joe's Turn
runners.spotlight: Course and distance winner in May off 9lb lower who has won twice (once over fences) and placed once from four visits here; finished last year with going close at Tipperary; well beaten on return at Punchestown last month but that was his first run in nine months so entitled to come on for it; reliable around here and big chance if he can progress from first run of the year.
runners.comment: Won twice from four visits here; should progress from return lto and big chance if so

runners.name: Dearg Mor
runners.spotlight: Fared better on second start in a handicap when beaten 15l into fourth at Wexford (3m, gd) 19 days ago in first-time cheekpieces; they're left on and he has each-way claims if building on that.
runners.comment: Fared better on second start in h'cap last month in cheekpieces; chance if building

runners.name: Move It On Over
runners.spotlight: Little impact in maiden hurdles and never a factor in two handicap starts this year; hopes pinned on step up in trip.
runners.comment: Beaten a fair way on both h'cap starts this year; needs improvement

runners.name: Everystep Oftheway
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; put together some positive runs in handicaps over staying trips when last seen but that was 707 days ago and he has it all to do after such a long absence.
runners.comment: Third reserve; positive runs when last seen but off 707 days; market can guide

runners.name: Blinding Light
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in four starts in maiden hurdles; big step up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off in four starts in maiden hurdles; big step up in trip on handicap debut

runners.name: Strong Contender
runners.spotlight: Nothing of note in four maiden hurdles; steps up in trip on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Nothing of note in four maiden hurdles; up in trip on handicap bow

runners.name: Fr Gilligansvoyge
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; veteran who is 1-70 under rules but he ran well twice in pair of handicap chases at Limerick and Tramore on good ground last month, finishing fourth and second; hasn't run over hurdles in nearly four years.
runners.comment: Second reserve; second at Tramore over fences lto; hasn't run over hurdles in a long time

runners.name: Derby Rock
runners.spotlight: Modest form in seven starts but took small step in right direction at this track a year ago when beaten 17.5l into fifth off 9lb higher; tailed off over hurdles and fences since; returns after 305 days off and needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Small step in right direction at this track in June but didn't progress; needs more

races.race_name: Follow Kilbeggan On Instagram Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: 0-44 but placed ten times over hurdles and stayed on to good effect at Sligo (2m5f, yld) six days ago; looked ready for this step back up in trip again and has claims.
runners.comment: Stayed on to good effect when third at Sligo six days ago; trip a question but good chance

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in a Cork maiden hurdle (2m7f, yld) in April but well beaten in two handicaps since; needs improvement in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran better on second start in h'cap lto but still beaten a fair way; needs improvement
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

runners.name: Gerrys Wish
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but was knocking on the door last year, going close at Perth and Galway over this trip on good and yielding ground; below form on return at Sligo 34 days ago but entitled to progress from that and she's off the same mark off which she was beaten a head at Galway in October; in top hands and one of the more likely ones.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door last year, placed three times; below form lto but should progress

runners.name: Katie's Casper
runners.spotlight: Showed a glimmer of promise when eighth in a maiden hurdle in December at Thurles (2m, yld) but has been well-held in two handicaps since, including when stepping up in trip at Tramore last time; goes further in distance now dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: No impression in two h'cap runs but maiden hurdle run at Thurles wasn't devoid of promise

runners.name: Chasing Crowns
