runners.spotlight: Went close at Down Royal (3m, gd) off 1lb lower in October but failed to build on that in two starts since on soft; return to good ground could bring about an improved showing, however, after 165 days off.
runners.comment: Went close at Down Royal in Oct; failed to build on that two starts since; off 165 days

runners.name: Charlie's Gift
runners.spotlight: Finished a decent third, albeit well-held, here last year over 2m3f off 9lb higher; failed to build on that since; midfield at Clonmel on return and fell two out last time at Limerick when losing ground; tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Failed to build on decent third here in July; midfield at Clonmel and fell lto; ew claims

runners.name: Riggs
runners.spotlight: Easy wins at Down Royal and Wexford (3m1f, gd) over fences last year and decent runs in defeat after; pulled up early at Wexford last month and bit to prove back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Easy winner at Wexford last year off 5lb lower; PU last month, entitled to come on for it
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Lily Milan
runners.spotlight: Produced a pair of useful efforts in bumpers but has failed to deliver on that promise over hurdles and was well beaten upped to this trip at Wexford 19 days ago; cheekpieces reached for.
runners.comment: Useful form in bumpers; failed to deliver over hurdles in three goes; needs more

runners.name: Brother Hollie
runners.spotlight: Little promise in first four starts but fared better on second start at this track in handicap company when beaten 12.5l into eighth behind Blue Reed over 2m3f; this trip likely within reach but needs improvement.
runners.comment: Fared better on second start in handicap lto here; needs more over this trip

runners.name: Stonecarthy
runners.spotlight: Standout run in 12 efforts came over 2m1f at Wexford last year when 3.75l second off 6lb higher; never a factor on return 38 days ago and needs to prove he stays this distance.
runners.comment: Runner-up over shorter at Wexford last year; never a factor on return; steps up in trip
runners.owner: Dean Brennan & Nigel Brennan & Ms Alana McGuinness

runners.name: Imperial Tune
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is 0-21 but was placed on heavy twice last year; below that form three times since on drier ground.
runners.comment: Veteran who was placed on heavy twice last year; below that form on drier ground since

runners.name: Not So Serious
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in bumpers but has disappointed in four starts over hurdles and hard to have confidence in.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability in bumpers but poor in maiden hurdles and no impact on h'cap debut lto

runners.name: The Ducks Walk
runners.spotlight: Step in right direction on third start when beaten 25l in maiden hurdle at Tramore (2m6f, gd) 16 days ago; steps up in trip on handicap debut from potentially lenient mark.
runners.comment: Step in right direction when seventh in maiden hurdle lto; could be off a lenient mark

runners.name: Back The West
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 0-25 and pulled up on two starts in handicaps this year; doesn't inspire confidence.
runners.comment: First reserve; pulled up twice this year in h'caps; hard to have confidence in

runners.name: Annie's Belle
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 2m3f at this track 15 days ago when beaten 7l into third by Blue Reed; leading chance if handling extra distance.
runners.comment: Progressed stepping up in trip lto when third; shapes as if will stay this trip; chance

runners.name: Smackwater Jack
runners.spotlight: Showed a good attitude to gain third success over hurdles when holding off Morell River by a neck at Punchestown (23.5f, gd) eight days ago; not an obvious type to follow up but he was 13lb out of the handicap that day so is effectively just 2lb higher here; chance of following up.
runners.comment: Good attitude to get third win at Punch eight days ago; 2lb rise very fair, chance again

runners.name: Stretchemout
runners.spotlight: Sligo beginners chase winner in October over 2m5f on yielding; failed to build on that in three subsequent runs but could be on a nice mark over hurdles starting out for new yard.
runners.comment: Third reserve; Sligo beginners chase winner in Oct; could be on a nice mark over hurdles

runners.name: Youknowme
runners.spotlight: Hasn't found improvement since switching to handicaps, well-held on both starts; hard to fancy from basement mark.
runners.comment: Well beat in two h'cap runs, last time up to this trip; needs more from basement mark

runners.name: Dylan Oak
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; won handicap hurdle at Roscommon (2m, gd) in 2022 and wasn't disgraced in three runs returning from a long absence in 2024; third in point-to-point when last seen and could figure.
runners.comment: Second reserve; not disgraced in three runs in 2024; third in ptp last seen; could figure

races.race_name: Paul & Carmel Fay Memorial Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cobra Queen
runners.spotlight: Rated 10lb higher over fences; hasn't been seen over hurdles since April of last year when tailed off at Punchestown and only 2lb lower now; pulled up in Mayo National on latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Rated 10lb higher over fences, pulled up in Mayo National on latest, others appeal more

runners.name: Crossing The Bar
runners.spotlight: 4-26 over hurdles; posted a solid effort when last seen at Fairyhouse in April 2025 and is now 3lb lower; three of four wins have come on soft ground, however, so rain would boost prospects.
runners.comment: Off since solid Fairyhouse run in April 2025, 3lb lower now, would prefer softer ground

runners.name: Toll Stone
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; no win over hurdles since Fairyhouse maiden success in February 2024 (2m, heavy); shaped ok when staying on for 16.5l fifth behind Jacovec Cavern on seasonal return but didn't back it up when 5-1 favourite at Ballinrobe (22.5f, good) latest; stamina to prove now up in trip.
runners.comment: Pleasing run at Punchestown, didn't back it up at Ballinrobe, stamina a query up in trip

runners.name: Rexem
runners.spotlight: 3-9 over hurdles; rediscovered form with fine chase efforts at Musselburgh in January but then pulled up in Ulster National and lifeless effort on return to this sphere at Kelso when beaten 28l; risky proposition.
runners.comment: In good form earlier this year over fences, way below best last twice, risky proposition

runners.name: Littlebiggie
runners.spotlight: Killarney mares' maiden hurdle winner in May 2024; added a 2m7f Ballinrobe mares' beginners' chase (soft) last September; runner up off higher marks in last two handicap hurdle starts (in 2024) but off since pulling up in a Thurles handicap chase in January; probably best watched.
runners.comment: First h'cap hurdle run since 2024, pulled up over fences latest, lacks a recent run, watch

runners.name: Moonovercloon
runners.spotlight: No win since scoring at Sligo (20.5f, good) in August 2023; rated 12lb higher over fences but failed to exploit lower hurdles mark on last four starts in 2025 and has now joined a new yard; lacks a run and best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Failed to exploit lower hurdles mark when last seen, new yard now, check the market

runners.name: Lisnamult Lad
runners.spotlight: Has lost way since a superb second in the 2025 Topham Chase at Aintree; three non-completions from four starts since and hopes now pinned on return to hurdling off 10lb lower mark sparking something.
runners.comment: Failed to complete three of last four starts, looks to exploit 10lb lower hurdles' mark

runners.name: Ballybow
runners.spotlight: 2-6 over hurdles including a Grade 3 novice; failed to make an impact in six chase starts and not much encouragement to take from return to this discipline at Punchestown in April; handicapper giving him a chance but needs to get back to form.
runners.comment: 2-6 over hurdles, failed to make an impact chasing, assesor giving him a chance

runners.name: St Lawrence's Well
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner (19.5f, good) has yet to show a whole lot over hurdles; best effort came in a four runner novice at Cork (3m good) when a 15l second to stablemate Tackletommywoowoo; not obviously well treated for handicap debut and also lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner, not obviously well treated for h'cap debut, lacks recent run

runners.name: Encanto Bruno
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back to 2023 and two runs since coming back to this yard have not suggested that a return to winning ways is imminent; beaten 9.25l when third in four runner race at Clonmel latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: On long losing run, modest third in four runner race on latest, others preferred

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good) off 6lb higher last May; won off this mark at Ballinrobe in August; below best over fences and hurdles recently, however, and needs to revive.
runners.comment: C&D winner, capable off this mark but below best recently, needs a revival

runners.name: Dorans Law
runners.spotlight: Trip and ground are fine for this hurdle and chase winner but hard to fancy on 2026 form; pulled up in the Ulster National and over the banks at Punchestown; beaten 24l when completing in Limerick handicap chase latest; can only watch back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Badly out of form in 2026, can only be watched now back over hurdles

runners.name: Jacovec Cavern
runners.spotlight: In fine form; won by 11l off 12lb lower at Punchestown (19.5f, yielding); creditable 8l second of 16 off revised mark at Ballinrobe (22.5f, good), staying on well; step up in trip looks a good move on that evidence and should go well again off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Won easily at Punchestown, stayed on well over 2m6f latest, go well again up in trip

runners.name: In Excelsis Deo
runners.spotlight: Smart chaser for Harry Fry; two point-to-point wins for current yard this year; midfield in Listowel hunter chase on latest (beaten 18.5l); more needed back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Two point wins this year, beaten 18.5l in hunter chase latest, needs more back hurdling

races.race_name: Midlands National On 10th July (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Streamhill
runners.spotlight: Modest form in point-to-points; definite promise in first two bumper runs for Terence O'Brien, including when a 5.5l fifth of 14 over C&D in April (gd-yld, had a subsequent bumper winner behind); soft ground a possible excuse for below par run on stable debut latest; could rebound back on sounder surface.
runners.comment: Promise in first two bumpers inc' over C&D, soft ground excuse latest, could rebound

runners.name: O'Callaghan Can
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on debut at Thurles (2m, soft) in February; much more like it when a 5.75l third of 10 last time over this C&D (15.5f, gd-yld); thereabouts with a repeat of that.
runners.comment: Much better effort when third over C&D last time, thereabouts with repeat of that

runners.name: Hartes Angels
runners.spotlight: Wings Of Eagles mare; half-sister to smart 2m3f/2m4f hurdle/chase winner Nickle Back; dam bumper, 7f and (RPR 68) and useful 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner; hooded and tongue tied for debut; check market.
runners.comment: Wings Of Eagles mare, dam bumper winner, hooded and tongue tied, check market

runners.name: Mistakenforanother
runners.spotlight: Debut run in a Clonmel bumper (16.5f, gd-yld) last month not devoid of promise but was still beaten 18.5l into fourth; will need to take a step forward to make an impact here but that's not impossible.
runners.comment: Debut fourth not devoid of promise, step forward needed here but not discounted

runners.name: Ocovango Lady
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on debut in a point-to-point in March 2025; still in contention when falling two out in another point 99 days ago; tongue tie on for Rules' debut; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Still in contention when falling two out in point latest, tongue tie on, best watched

runners.name: Johnny Rambo
runners.spotlight: Outran odds of 40-1 when a 3.5l second of 17 behind a 98 rated rival in a 4yo Clonmel maiden hurdle (16.5f, good) on debut 10 days ago; should be in the mix if building on that promising start.
runners.comment: Outran odds when second in maiden hurdle, in the mix if building on that pleasing start

runners.name: Days Gone By
runners.spotlight: Pour Moi mare; dam point winner, half-sister to 2m7f hurdle winner Oaks Soldier, out of bumper and 2m1f/2m4f hurdle winner; likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Pour Moui mare, dam point winner, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Charlie Shiron
runners.spotlight: 4yo No Risk At All filly; half-sister to French 2m3f hurdle winner Noubaka; dam pulled up only start over hurdles, half-sister to winners Accelerator (2m1f-2m3f hurdle/chase) and Blueness (French 2m3f hurdle); watching brief advised.
runners.comment: No Risk At All filly, half-sister to 2m3f French hurdle winner, watching brief advised

runners.name: Sea Feliz
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Getaway mare; E19,000 3yo; half-sister to 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Morricone; dam bumper winner, half-sister to useful bumper and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Encore Champs; top yard and likely to go well if gets in.
runners.comment: Getaway mare, dam bumper winner, top yard, should go well if gets a run; reserve

runners.name: I'm Gold
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 4yo Order Of St George filly; dam runner-up in point only start, closely related to 2m/2m1f hurdle winner As Tears Go By, half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Black Tear; a bit of appeal on paper; check market.
runners.comment: Order Of St George filly, dam related to bumper winner, check market; reserve

runners.name: Chapel Gate
runners.spotlight: Order Of St George gelding didn't achieve much in three completed point-to-points; cheekpieces on for Rules' debut.
runners.comment: Modest form in completed points, cheekpieces on for Rules' debut

runners.name: Colossal Impact
runners.spotlight: Had a subsequent dual-hurdles winner behind when making a pleasing debut in a Navan bumper in March (2m, soft); pedigree suggests he should improve for better ground; solid claims.
runners.comment: Pleasing debut at Navan, should improve for better ground on breeding, solid chance
runners.owner: J J Leckey & Donal P Keane & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Discrete
runners.spotlight: Remote third on point-to-point debut but failed to complete three of four starts since; pedigree suggests he'll need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Failed to complete three of five starts in points, bred to need further in time

runners.name: Grabat
runners.spotlight: Bathyrhon gelding; brother to French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle winner Grandbat; dam unraced half-sister to bumper/useful 2m hurdle winner French Crusader; yard 1-53 in bumpers last five seasons; likely best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Brother to French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle winner Grandbat, likely best watched on debut

runners.name: Rememberwhat
runners.spotlight: Elusive Pimpernel gelding; dam unplaced in bumpers/2m6f hurdle, sister to bumper and smart 2m1f-3m hurdle/chase winner Lots Of Memories, half-sister to bumper winner He'llberemembered; yard had winner on the Flat last week; check market.
runners.comment: Elusive Pimpernel gelding, dam sister to bumper winner, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Tommy's Ticket
runners.spotlight: Jet Away gelding; twice-raced dam closely related to 2m3f chase winner Toohighforme, half-sister to 3m hurdle winner Let The Show Begin; yard not known for success in this sphere; watch.
runners.comment: Jet Away gelding, dam related to two chase winners, best watched

runners.name: Long Story
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and soundly beaten on debut in a Ballinrobe bumper; may need more time.
runners.comment: 66-1 and well beaten on debut at Ballinrobe, may need more time

runners.name: Tale Of Intrigue
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at big odds in two Punchestown bumpers last month; unlikely winner.
runners.comment: Tailed off at long odds both starts in Punchestown bumpers last month, unlikely

runners.name: Pamelaisbrass
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; tailed off on heavy ground in a Naas bumper on debut in February when 100-1; could improve for better ground but can only be watched.
runners.comment: Tailed off on heavy on debut at Naas, can only be watched for now; reserve

races.race_name: Find Us At fitzdares.com EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Enemy Lines
runners.spotlight: Price dropped to 1,600gns the second time he was solid; however, seems a bargain purchase having shown promise with second of nine in Doncaster maiden (5f, good to firm) that has thrown up a couple of subsequent winners; leading player.
runners.comment: Second of nine in Doncaster race that has thrown up two subsequent winners; leading claims

runners.name: Holliesthedollie
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly; took a step forward at Lingfield (6f, AW; fourth of ten) while shaping as if this drop to 5f is worth exploring; possibilities back on turf.
runners.comment: Took a step forward last time while shaping as if this drop to 5f is worth exploring

runners.name: Our Fella
runners.spotlight: Has started 66-1 in both outings but belied those odds with third of ten over C&D (good to firm) latest; one of the main form contenders, assuming that effort is backed up.
runners.comment: 66-1 in both starts but belied those odds with third of ten over C&D latest

runners.name: Curious Minx
runners.spotlight: Dream Ahead half-sister to US 1m-1m3f turf winner City Exile; dam multiple 6f winner (RPR 88), half-sister to Listed-placed Italian 5f-7f winner Dorkhel, out of 5f-6f winner; represents major yard that has a good record at Windsor; one of the likelier types.
runners.comment: Dream Ahead filly; represents major yard that has a good record at Windsor

runners.name: Tree Wizard
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who dropped away in seller most recently, failing to build on his close second in Beverley claimer (5f); wore cheekpieces both times; chance depends on the effects of a switch to blinkers.
runners.comment: Best effort in claimer; chance depends on the effects of new headgear

runners.name: Star Bayside Boy
runners.spotlight: Bayside Boy filly; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to two useful winners over 1m-1m4f, out of 1m 2yo winning sister to multiple 1m-1m4f Group/Grade 1 winner St Nicholas Abbey; notable connections; bred to need further but market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Bayside Boy filly; notable connections; heed the market signals

runners.name: Minster Boy
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling; Calyx half-brother to five winners, including some as 2yos, notably I'm Spartacus (6f AW; RPR 75); dam 5.7f 2yo winner (88); in good hands and looks a likely type on breeding.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling; in good hands and looks a likely type on breeding

runners.name: Emirates Express
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished last of ten in C&D contest; needs a huge step forward.
runners.comment: Last of ten in C&D contest; brings the worst form

runners.name: Blake's Lass
runners.spotlight: Bred to do well as a 2yo; duly made a positive start, amid greenness, in maiden at Redcar (5f, good to firm) where she finished 1l second of seven having attracted support; open to progress and warrants respect.
runners.comment: Made a positive start, amid greenness, in Redcar event having attracted support; respected
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Sheer Beauty
runners.spotlight: 23,000euros yearling, 15,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Kodi Bear and first foal of 1m AW winning (RPR 82) daughter of 1m 2yo winning half-sister to Derby winner Oath; market informative.
runners.comment: 15,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Kodi Bear out of 1m AW winner; market helpful

runners.name: Crowned
runners.spotlight: 31,000euros yearling, £5,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force; dam once-raced half-sister to US stakes-placed 8.5f winner Tarneema, out of unraced sister to US 1m1f/1m2f Grade 2 winner Malibu Pier; yard's record with 2yos this term features a winning newcomer (SP 16-1).
runners.comment: £5,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Persian Force; yard has had a winning 2yo newcomer this term

runners.name: Hailstones In May
runners.spotlight: Sold for 4,500euros then 15,500euros as a yearling; Space Blues half-brother to winners Wonderful Thing (1m; RPR 58) and Golden Vega (Italian 1m 2yo); dam Kazakhstani 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Musharakaat; one of two newcomers for his stable; market instructive.
runners.comment: 15,500euros yearling; by Space Blues; one of two newcomers for his stable

runners.name: It's A Dream
runners.spotlight: Carried left in the latter stages and faded into fifth of 12 in 6f maiden on fast ground at Windsor; recorded a modest RPR but may do better dropped to 5f with the outing under his belt.
runners.comment: Faded into fifth over 6f at Windsor; may do better dropped to 5f
runners.owner: P J & Mrs J P Haycock

runners.name: Space Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Faded into fifth of ten, having made the running, in C&D event (good to firm); finished about 2l behind Our Fella who had the benefit of experience; may see her race out better this time and consequently improve.
runners.comment: Faded into fifth of ten over C&D; may see her race out better this time

races.race_name: Fitzdares Telephone & Text Betting Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Egyptian Pharaoh
runners.spotlight: Placed at Dundalk (1m/7f, AW) in the last two of his three starts for Kevin Coleman; gelded since the final occasion; now goes in tongue-tie; bred to stay this new trip; leading form claims.
runners.comment: Gelded since final Irish start; leading player on his Dundalk AW form

runners.name: Zabeel Small
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in 8.5f AW event at Wolverhampton, beating all bar a 1-14 shot who took record to 2-2; likely to stay this longer distance and looks open to progress; respected.
runners.comment: Showed promise behind 1-14 shot in AW maiden; open to progress; respected

runners.name: Cold Fish
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts over 1m2f on AW at Chelmsford and 1m on good ground at Goodwood, finishing third both times; has solid-looking place claims back up in distance.
runners.comment: Third in both starts, latest at Goodwood; has solid-looking place claims

runners.name: Adalo
runners.spotlight: Never landed a blow in 1m maiden at Goodwood (behind Cold Fish) but may improve with the experience under his belt; bred to be suited by this longer distance.
runners.comment: May improve with Goodwood effort (behind Cold Fish) under his belt

runners.name: Spinney
runners.spotlight: One of the better form contenders, having finished fourth at Bath (1m2f) last time, but likely needs further progress to get off the mark.
runners.comment: One of the better form contenders but likely needs progress to win

runners.name: Clarissa Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced filly who has weak claims on form; handicaps should be more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Has weak claims on form

runners.name: Digger One
runners.spotlight: Greatly inferior to Bankatary on bumper form but is plausibly open to improvement in this new code, being half-brother to a multiple Flat winner for his connections.
runners.comment: Greatly inferior to Bankatary on bumper form but may do better in this sphere

runners.name: Bankatary
runners.spotlight: Flat debutant who looks a possible danger on his bumper form, which comprises a win at Huntingdon and creditable third at Ludlow, both on good ground last autumn.
runners.comment: Flat debutant who looks a possible danger on bumper form, which includes a win

runners.name: Cormorant Rock
runners.spotlight: Needs improvement on his Lingfield efforts, modest fourth over 7.5f latest; steps up in trip.
runners.comment: Needs improvement on his Lingfield efforts

runners.name: Sunnie Jim
runners.spotlight: Dismal claims on his 1m efforts; combination of new trip and first-time headgear needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Dismal claims on his 1m efforts

runners.name: Ice To Meet You
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having trailed home last of nine in 7f turf maiden at Lingfield on belated debut.
runners.comment: Trailed home last of nine in 7f maiden at Lingfield

runners.name: Roi De Coeur
runners.spotlight: Modest chance on his AW efforts at about 1m; this new scenario needs to prompt something better.
runners.comment: New scenario needs to prompt a bigger effort

runners.name: Fabled Spirit
runners.spotlight: Down the field in both outings; handicaps should be more suitable after this.
runners.comment: Down the field in both outings

races.race_name: Fitzdares Sprint Series Qualifier Handicap

runners.name: Uncle Don
runners.spotlight: Still seeking a first handicap success but he has gone close in two of his three runs for Robert Cowell, beaten a short head over C&D three weeks ago (good to firm); slower ground would be no issue but he will need a bit more to defy this 2lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Threatening for new yard, including over C&D; edging up the weights but should run well

runners.name: Fleetwater
runners.spotlight: Well suited by this track, recording both her wins here and in the frame on her other three visits; ran on well to take third at Goodwood eight days ago; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Good record over C&D (12431); solid 3rd at Goodwood eight days ago; one to take seriously
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Another Abbot
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f last summer; promising seasonal return at Ascot (5f) last month and duly stepped forward with a hard-fought Yarrmouth win 18 days ago, pulling a long way clear with a rival who has since bolted up; still improving and a 5lb rise shouldn't prevent another big run.
runners.comment: Pulled a long way clear with a subsequent easy winner at Yarmouth; big run on the cards

runners.name: Durham Castle
runners.spotlight: Justified short-priced favouritism in the corresponding event 12 months ago off a 3lb lower mark; absent until returning at Kempton in March, a race where he was well supported but weakened in the final furlong; still has low mileage.
runners.comment: Won this race last year; may have needed the run on Kempton return in March; low mileage

runners.name: Rajaking
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; blew hot and cold for former stable but well handicapped on 2025 best; raced freely and dropped away in the final furlong on his stable debut at Newmarket last month; up in class and likely vulnerable.
runners.comment: C&D winner; sharper for last month's stable debut but vulnerable upped in grade

runners.name: Change Sings
runners.spotlight: Over two years since the last of his five wins; shaped well at Kempton on his reappearance but he hasn't built on it in two runs since; broke out of the stalls and withdrawn when favourite for a Kempton handicap last month; well drawn to attack and he lurks on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: On losing run but he retains ability and is on a dangerous mark; well drawn to attack too

runners.name: Diligently
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap off this mark last August and when second at Thirsk the following month he split rivals who won the Portland and Ayr Bronze Cup on their next starts; beaten favourite over C&D five weeks ago but he had a torrid run through in a messy race; drawn widest but he's well handicapped.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a good mark and had excuses here last month; one to take seriously

runners.name: Toolatetonegotiate
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly who is going from strength to strength this year, comfortably seeing off five rivals in a Class 4 fillies' handicap at Bath last month; a 7lb rise in a stronger race demands another step forward.
runners.comment: Thriving filly but 7lb rise for latest small-field win leaves her vulnerable at this level

runners.name: Amazonian Dream
runners.spotlight: Third to Durham Castle in this race 12 months ago off an 11lb higher mark but only six ran there and this is considerably deeper; ran okay in a first-time visor at Kempton last month but others appeal more today.
runners.comment: 11lb lower than when 3rd in corresponding race last year but this is a stronger renewal

runners.name: Alpha Magic
runners.spotlight: Two 6f wins to his name on good to soft ground; unplaced in two runs this year but neither run was without promise; he will need a personal best to come out on top here though.
runners.comment: Met trouble in running at Hamilton latest (5f); can win off this mark but maybe not here

runners.name: Shiplake
runners.spotlight: Two impressive 7f wins on AW this winter; not so good in deeper company back on turf last month but he drops back in trip and doesn't look impossibly treated.
runners.comment: Impressed twice on AW (7f) this winter; down field in good h'caps last month; back at 6f

runners.name: Sturlasson
runners.spotlight: Did well for new yard last autumn, winning three (5f, AW/good to soft), and he has run some solid races in defeat in 2026, including over this trip at Goodwood eight days ago (soft); likely vulnerable to stronger stayers.
runners.comment: Retains ability but he may prove vulnerable to stronger stayers this evening

runners.name: Righthere Rightnow
runners.spotlight: 0-12 since his wide-margin debut win as a 2yo but he ended 2025 with some good runs and went close in Dubai in December (6f, good to firm); his last two runs at Meydan weren't his best but a break may have freshened him up and he is on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: 0-12 since his debut win but down in weights and retains ability; not discounted

runners.name: Indian Run
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on last spring's Newmarket/Newbury form; down the field in both runs this year but he was awkward at the start on his reappearance and not well drawn at York last time; unwise to underestimate him now he drops in class.
runners.comment: Drops in class off a dangerous mark and there have been excuses so far this year

races.race_name: Fitzdares Handicap

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt with a front-running Wetherby success 11 days ago (5.5f, good to firm); the runner-up has since won easily (pair clear) and the Topspeed figure was good for the grade; should go well despite her 6lb rise.
runners.comment: Made all at Wetherby 11 days ago and the runner-up has won easily since; should go well

runners.name: Mister Moet
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins for Scott Dixon this winter and he is just 1lb higher than for March's 5f success; finished last at Lingfield on Good Friday but that was a stiff task and this is much more suitable; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Well beaten on Good Friday but this is much more suitable; one to take seriously

runners.name: Rosieisme Darling
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW over the winter and she has run two solid races back on turf this summer; suspicion a few of these will have her measure tonight though.
runners.comment: Arrives in good form but suspicion a few of her rivals will prove better treated

runners.name: Viking Glory
runners.spotlight: Promise, albeit expensive to follow, for Adrian Keatley over the winter; disappointing in two turf handicaps for James Owen last month but not drops into a Class 6; hood experiment quickly aborted.
runners.comment: Two disappointing runs in handicaps for James Owen last month; now drops to Class 6

runners.name: Solar Invincible
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with a 5f win at Chelmsford in December; added a turf success at Bath in April (5f, good) and he is only 2lb higher today; two defeats since have come in stronger company and Billy Loughnane has been on board for both wins (from three rides on him); good chance.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins since handicapping, both when ridden by Billy Loughnane; down in class; solid

runners.name: Rianka
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three 6f runs in novice/maiden company, running her best race here two weeks ago (good to firm); tongue tied for handicap debut and dropping to 5f could help too; betting useful.
runners.comment: Improvement required now handicapping over new trip but it's not impossible; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Runamara
runners.spotlight: On a reduced mark; ran well when second at Kempton (6f) in April but he has failed to build on that promise twice since; others stronger.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on his Kempton second (6f) in two runs since; others look stronger

runners.name: Bella Delizia
runners.spotlight: Improved form when justifying support with a brave win on nursery debut at Redcar (5f, good; 2-1) in October; well beaten on her reappearance in April but she should be seen to better effect today; Tom Marquand booked.
runners.comment: Redcar win on nursery debut last October; well held on return but can do better today

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: Third of five at Catterick on his handicap debut last month (6f, good; tongue tied first time); mark left unchanged and he will need more for the return to 5f.
runners.comment: Third of five on last month's handicap debut; neeeds more back at 5f but low mileage

runners.name: Truly Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who made an encouraging seasonal/stable debut when fourth of ten over C&D six weeks ago; that was his first start for 233 days and he remains capable of better; high on the list.
runners.comment: Unexposed sprinter who shaped well over C&D six weeks ago; high on the list

runners.name: Little Jaybee
runners.spotlight: Not matched 2yo best in three runs this year; latest Wolverhampton sixth was a small step back in the right direction and she now drops into her first Class 6 handicap; not discounted.
runners.comment: Yet to shine this year but more promise last time and now drops into her first Class 6
runners.owner: Mrs A L Lofts & Mrs Nicky Scott Knight

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & Dab Handicap

runners.name: Hayynah
runners.spotlight: AW winner; below par returned to turf last time but may rebound in this scenario, having finished second in both C&D attempts.
runners.comment: May rebound, having finished second in both C&D attempts

runners.name: Echo Of Faith
runners.spotlight: 7.5f winner for Jessica Harrington; sold for 16,000gns since Listed defeat last time; something to prove back down in distance.
runners.comment: 7.5f winner in Ireland; something to prove back down in trip

runners.name: Alasrae
runners.spotlight: Progress on AW this year features three wins; failed to transfer the form back to turf last time, only midfield at Hamilton; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to transfer AW form back to turf last time; bit to prove

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: Produced one of his lesser efforts in C&D event last time and remains a maiden; chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Marcellinus
runners.spotlight: Has a consistent record, latterly winning at Bath (5.7f) and second at Hamilton (6f) since switched to handicaps, both on good ground; solid.
runners.comment: Winner at Bath and second at Hamilton since handicapping; solid

runners.name: Got The Booty
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has shown peak RPRs when runner-up twice on AW; progress isn't guaranteed on somewhat belated handicap debut.
runners.comment: Best form when runner-up twice on AW; handicap debut

runners.name: Shahik
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his 7f form this term for new yard but may prove resurgent back at 6f in first-time blinkers; gained his 2yo win over this trip.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent back at 6f in first-time blinkers

runners.name: Calling A Star
runners.spotlight: Made all in AW maiden in most recent 6f attempt and the return to this trip looks a plus; raced too freely over 7f last time.
runners.comment: Made all in AW maiden in most recent 6f attempt
runners.owner: A Smith, J Henwood & Star Amusements

runners.name: Our Guy
runners.spotlight: Suited by the drop to 6f at Windsor (good to firm) last time on handicap/turf debut; may well build on that win and improve further; respected.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor last time on handicap/turf debut; may well improve further

runners.name: Riley Rocks
runners.spotlight: Has failed to transfer his AW progress to turf in two attempts (5f) but may prove resurgent back up in distance, being 2-2 over 6f.
runners.comment: May prove resurgent, being 2-2 over 6f (both wins on AW)

runners.name: Born Slippy
runners.spotlight: Lacks recent match practice and has gone up 4lb for a final 2yo effort that may flatter him; gained his sole win off 9lb lower.
runners.comment: Has gone up 4lb for a final 2yo effort that may flatter him

runners.name: Akabusi
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-18; creditable fourth over C&D (good to firm) last time when bidding to follow up his Newmarket success; remains in form.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth over C&D last time when bidding to follow up a win

runners.name: Visually
runners.spotlight: Unexposed contender whose 5f novice form (including debut win) last October has substance; gelded since; extra furlong should be fine; interesting.
runners.comment: Unexposed contender whose 2yo novice form has substance; interesting

races.race_name: Fitzdares Taking Bets Since 1882 Handicap

runners.name: Seventy
runners.spotlight: Building up a solid record for current stable and was beaten only narrowly by Mrembo over C&D (good to firm) last time when bidding for a Windsor double; major contender.
runners.comment: Beaten only narrowly by Mrembo over C&D last time when bidding for a Windsor double

runners.name: Big Bear Hug
runners.spotlight: Placed at Lingfield (11.5f) last time, proving suited by return to turf and showing clear signs of a return to form; remains 12lb below last winning mark; possibilities.
runners.comment: Placed at Lingfield last time; remains 12lb below last winning mark; possibilities

runners.name: Jimmy Mark
runners.spotlight: Five wins under the care of Stuart Kittow; creditable second at Bath on seasonal debut; has filled the same position in both attempts over C&D; again has place claims.
runners.comment: Creditable second at Bath on reappearance; has filled same position in both C&D runs

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, including latest start (good to firm) when narrowly beating Seventy in three-runner field; back up only 1lb and looks likely to remain competitive.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, including latest start when narrowly beating Seventy; shortlisted

runners.name: Victors Spirit
runners.spotlight: Should benefit from reapplied hood having been too free returned to turf last time; has shown consistent AW form in this headgear, initiated by 1m3f success in April.
runners.comment: Has consistent AW form in a hood, which is reapplied this evening
runners.owner: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership

runners.name: Stipulation
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent sort on turf/AW at up to 1m3f, albeit gets little respite from the assessor; form dipped last time but he may well rebound and pick up prize-money.
runners.comment: May rebound from below-par effort last time; generally consistent otherwise
runners.owner: Horn And Trumpet Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Educator
runners.spotlight: Defied an 8lb higher mark over C&D in May 2025 but was in better form at the time; chance depends on the effects of a belated third attempt in blinkers (last worn in 2023).
runners.comment: Won off 8lb higher over C&D in May 2025 but was in better form at the time

runners.name: Mister Daydream
runners.spotlight: Three wins for previous yard; ran according to market expectations (rank outsider of seven) at Brighton on stable/seasonal debut; the betting may again give guidance.
runners.comment: Last of seven at Brighton on stable/seasonal debut when the rank outsider

runners.name: Seeking Charles
runners.spotlight: Maiden who is far from solid on Flat/hurdles form for current yard but whose record suggests this return to a Flat turf race is a plus.
runners.comment: Maiden who is far from solid on form for current yard

races.race_name: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Handicap

runners.name: Too Hot To Tango
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent maiden whose efforts over further of late suggest she may show improvement for this return to 1m; possibilities off a reduced mark in third handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Generally consistent maiden; may show improvement for drop back to 1m; possibilities

runners.name: Zipwire
runners.spotlight: Broadly consistent in handicaps, mostly on AW, until below par returned to turf last time when too free over 1m2f; return to 1m looks a good move but he's still seeking first turf win.
runners.comment: Return to 1m looks a good move but he's still seeking first turf win
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Ziggy's Avenger
runners.spotlight: Good third at Kempton (AW) and successful at Ripon (good) since switched to handicap level, both in Class 6 over 1m; now 4lb higher and upped in grade but may improve further; highly respected.
runners.comment: Good third at Kempton and successful at Ripon since handicapping; may improve further
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc

runners.name: Espona Bay
runners.spotlight: Has consistent AW form, latterly for new yard; narrowly beat Court Of Stars at Wolverhampton (7f) most recently; possible danger, provided he's as effective back on turf and at new trip.
runners.comment: Has consistent AW form; narrowly beat Court Of Stars at Wolverhampton most recently

runners.name: Twilight Glow
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent maiden who has raced only over 6f/7f; doesn't appear to be crying out for this new trip; others preferred.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden who isn't crying out for this new trip

runners.name: Upsomdowns
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame a few times on AW but completely failed to transfer the form to turf in C&D event most recently, finishing last of eight; something to prove.
runners.comment: Completely failed to transfer AW form to turf in C&D event last time

runners.name: Claytons Kolatonic
runners.spotlight: Best effort (on AW) may not be reliable form; did nothing to alter that impression on handicap/turf debut last time.
runners.comment: Just a modest effort on handicap/turf debut last time

runners.name: Court Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Record of 122, all over 7f on AW, since handicapping; beaten by a stablemate, who won easily, last time; likely player provided she transfers the form back to turf and back to 1m.
runners.comment: Likely player provided she transfers 7f AW handicap form back to turf and back to 1m

runners.name: The Bureau Club
runners.spotlight: Four-race maiden who has failed to transfer her AW form to turf; effectively has something to prove off her mark on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Four-race maiden who has failed to transfer AW form to turf; handicap debut

runners.name: Flyta
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only on AW; rank outsider and well held on handicap debut most recently, failing to back up her January maiden efforts; chance depends on the effects of switching to turf.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of switching to turf
